assets.name: Secure Bootloader
assets.description: Primary bootloader responsible for verifying the authenticity and integrity of firmware images before execution on any compute tier (HPC, Gateway, Zonal Controller, or Classic ECU).

safety.rationale: Corporations do not suffer physical injuries.

financial.rationale: Compromise allows fleet-wide persistent rootkits, requiring massive physical recall and component replacement.

safety.rationale: A compromised bootloader can load malicious firmware into safety-critical ECUs, leading to loss of vehicle control.

assets.name: Device Private Key / Root of Trust
assets.description: Cryptographic private key securely fused in physical hardware (HSM, TPM, or TEE) used for component identity, secure boot, and cryptographic handshakes.

financial.rationale: Key compromise breaks the trust anchor, allowing attackers to forge legitimate signed firmware updates or clone physical modules.

safety.rationale: Enables malicious OTA updates to safety-critical components.

assets.name: Operating System Kernel
assets.description: The base runtime operating system layer (RTOS or Rich OS) managing hardware resources, execution tasks, and process isolation boundaries.

operational.rationale: Kernel compromise causes complete loss of control over the node's behavior.

safety.rationale: Kernel panic or hijacking in an RTOS can instantly freeze safety-critical actuator commands.

assets.name: Vehicle Access Authorization Token
assets.description: Ephemeral cryptographic security keys or tokens authorizing vehicle unlocking, physical entry, and immobilization override (covers PEPS, PaaK, and smart fobs).

financial.rationale: Results in reputational damage and potential liability for easily stolen vehicles.
Token sniffing or replay attacks bypass physical barriers, enabling complete vehicle theft.

assets.name: In-Vehicle User Profile and PII Data
assets.description: User-specific personal data stored locally within the vehicle compute environment, including navigation history, sync maps, contact directories, and cloud application credentials.

privacy.rationale: Large-scale data breaches compromise regulatory compliance frameworks (GDPR/CCPA), leading to severe fines.
Exposure of home addresses, travel patterns, and personal contact graphs.

assets.name: Machine Learning Perception Weights & Models
assets.description: Deep learning network configuration layers, object detection models, and neural network weights used to classify environmental features (lanes, signs, targets). Shared by specialized edge sensors or central ADAS accelerators.

financial.rationale: Exfiltration of proprietary neural network weights represents massive intellectual property loss for the OEM.

safety.rationale: Adversarial modifications or poisoning of model files leads to target misclassification, creating critical safety hazards.

assets.name: Automated Driving Trajectory Planning Algorithm
assets.description: Decision-making application binaries that ingest sensor fusion telemetry and output core dynamic lateral and longitudinal vehicle control matrices.

safety.rationale: Corruption of the trajectory logic directly commands the vehicle into unsafe physical paths.

assets.name: OTA Update Orchestration Middleware
assets.description: Software lifecycle middleware coordinating package download, security verification, signature verification, and flashing choreography across targeting sub-buses.

financial.rationale: Failure of OTA mechanisms prevents the OEM from patching security flaws or deploying feature updates, leading to mass physical recalls.

safety.rationale: A compromised OTA orchestrator allows arbitrary code execution across any ECU in the vehicle.

assets.name: Wireless Wide-Area Network (WWAN) Communication Channel
assets.description: The cellular radio frequency connection (4G/5G/6G) link establishing data transport pipelines between the vehicle's network coordinator and external telematics base stations.

operational.rationale: Loss of telemetry data from the fleet.

safety.rationale: Loss of cellular infrastructure breaks automated emergency tracking and safety eCall applications.

assets.name: Central Network Routing & Firewall Rules
assets.description: Configuration matrices, access control lists (ACLs), and frame forwarding rules separating security domains over CAN matrices or Ethernet VLANs.

safety.rationale: Corrupting security rules permits unauthorized lateral movement from low-trust domains to safety-critical target buses.

assets.name: Type-1 Hypervisor Platform
assets.description: Hardware-level hypervisor partitioning execution environments into safe, highly isolated virtual machines on high-performance compute nodes.

safety.rationale: Hypervisor jailbreaks permit an unprivileged OS guest (like Infotainment) to crash or starve real-time safety critical tasks running in adjacent VMs.

assets.name: OEM & Fleet Operations Back-End API Interface
assets.description: Web service gateway patterns allowing the vehicle platform to exchange operational metrics, state configurations, and remote orchestration events with cloud structures.

safety.rationale: Cloud APIs generally cannot directly command kinetic functions at speed due to gateway filtering.

operational.rationale: API manipulation or denial-of-service states degrade operational visibility across the entire vehicle fleet.
Loss of remote start, remote climate, or cloud-based navigation capabilities.

assets.name: Secure Non-Volatile Storage / Hardware Protected Partition
assets.description: Protected physical non-volatile system structures hosting core system binaries, persistent operational parameters, and localized encryption manifests.

safety.rationale: Corruption of NVRAM can brick ECU operations or load corrupted configuration states during driving.

assets.name: Hardware Enclave / Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) Core
assets.description: Physical processor isolating secure operations, ensuring private key processing and sensitive cryptographic computations are hidden from high-level software environments.

financial.rationale: Compromise of the core enclave drops trust verification boundaries, breaking fleet key isolation architectures.

assets.name: Vehicle Immobilization Cryptographic Secret
assets.description: Pre-shared master keys or security parameters validated across local coordination boundaries prior to authorizing powertrain system loop activation.

financial.rationale: Reputational impact and warranty/insurance claims due to easily bypassed immobilizers.
Key theft allows full physical bypass of anti-theft mechanics, enabling vehicle theft.

assets.name: Non-Safety Vehicle Comfort Configuration Profile
assets.description: Configuration metadata layouts managing peripheral user comfort bounds, actuator target mappings, and internal environmental preferences (e.g., HVAC, seating positions).

operational.rationale: Annoyance due to reset seating or temperature settings.

assets.name: Low-Speed Serial Local Interconnect Network (LIN) Bus Data Flow
assets.description: Low-frequency master-slave communication interactions managing peripheral interface monitoring, mechanical switches, and window/panel status states.

safety.rationale: Potential for minor mechanical pinching (windows/sunroof) if local hardware anti-pinch controls fail simultaneously.

assets.name: Driver Information Display Rendering Application
assets.description: High-priority graphic execution space responsible for rendering vital telemetry arrays (speedometer telemetry, active indicators, power levels) onto vehicle display blocks.

safety.rationale: Freezing or masking active screen outputs prevents the operator from identifying critical vehicle states or actual road velocities.

assets.name: Safety-Critical Telltale & Warning Signal Flow
assets.description: High-priority vehicle metric network traffic carrying structural fault markers, malfunction indicator states, and dynamic restraint statuses across architectural boundaries.

safety.rationale: Spoofing or dropping indicator signals blinds the road user to active platform faults, increasing operational risk profiles.

assets.name: Wireless Handset Pairing Session Keys
assets.description: Cryptographic link keys generated during mobile device pairing protocols (e.g., Bluetooth, Wi-Fi Direct) to ensure secure sessions between user devices and the vehicle's connection manager.

privacy.rationale: OEM liability for failing to secure Bluetooth link keys.
Key extraction allows an attacker to intercept personal calls, contacts, and media playback.

assets.name: High-Bandwidth Sensor Raw Telemetry Flow
assets.description: High-throughput data pipeline carrying raw or semi-processed environmental perception streams (cameras, LiDAR, Radar) from edge arrays into central fusion processors.

safety.rationale: Corporations do not suffer physical injuries.

financial.rationale: Safety failures due to blind ADAS systems result in massive litigation and recall costs.

safety.rationale: Dropping or corrupting sensor streams blinds automated driving systems, causing failure to react to physical obstacles.

assets.name: Active Safety Control Command Application
assets.description: Safety-critical application logic commanding immediate actuator corrections upon detecting imminent collision or lane departure vectors.

safety.rationale: Maliciously triggering or disabling this logic can force the vehicle to swerve or fail to brake in emergencies.

assets.name: System Diagnostics and Operational Log Files
assets.description: Runtime operational event records, diagnostic trace strings (e.g., DLT), crash dumps, and historical telemetry generated by underlying operating systems across any compute node.

privacy.rationale: Logs frequently capture plain-text location histories, API connections, and connected device identifiers.

assets.name: Cloud & Mobile App Authorization Token (OAuth/JWT)
assets.description: Bearer tokens or session cookies generated via cloud authentication used to authorize the mobile companion app and execute remote vehicle orchestration commands (e.g., remote start, unlock) via the backend.

safety.rationale: Cloud tokens cannot bypass kinetic safety gateways.

privacy.rationale: Token theft allows account takeover, exposing user location history and granting remote access.

assets.name: Vehicle-to-Cloud (V2C) PKI Identity Certificate
assets.description: Cryptographic identity document facilitating transport encryption (mTLS) and ensuring mutual authentication between vehicle endpoint nodes and OEM backend infrastructures.

financial.rationale: Compromising the certificate allows a threat actor to impersonate the vehicle to the cloud, injecting false fleet data.

assets.name: Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) Registry Middleware
assets.description: Dynamic service discovery and routing framework coordinating inter-process messaging (e.g., SOME/IP, DDS) across high-performance compute architectures and Ethernet boundaries.

safety.rationale: Hijacking the SOA registry allows routing safety-critical function calls to malicious ghost services.

assets.name: Containerized Service Encapsulation Profile
assets.description: Encapsulated user-space computing instances (e.g., Docker, LXC) isolating infotainment or telematics software services from the core hypervisor/kernel layers.

safety.rationale: A breakout targets the host OS but remains isolated from physical vehicle control lines.

assets.name: Diagnostic Security Access Authentication Secrets
assets.description: Seed/Key calculation algorithms and fixed secret arrays embedded within node firmware to authenticate privileged UDS diagnostic sessions (e.g., Service $27, Service $29).

financial.rationale: Extraction of UDS secrets allows unauthorized firmware flashing and parameter manipulation across the platform.

safety.rationale: Allows an attacker to actuate brakes or alter steering calibrations while driving.

assets.name: Third-Party API Keys & Service Credentials
assets.description: Static API keys or shared secrets hardcoded or provisioned in the Infotainment/Telematics systems to authenticate outbound requests to third-party cloud services (e.g., mapping providers, weather APIs, streaming media).

financial.rationale: Extraction of hardcoded API keys allows attackers to abuse OEM service quotas, causing massive unexpected cloud billing.

operational.rationale: Loss of service if the OEM revokes the compromised API key.

assets.name: Physical Key RF Rolling Code Seed Matrix
assets.description: Secret seed tracking parameters dynamically synchronizing cryptographically shifting authorization codes for physical access control modules.

financial.rationale: Extracting or successfully predicting the seed values enables trivial cloning of physical keys, leading to vehicle theft.

assets.name: Trust Anchor / OEM Root Certificate
assets.description: The foundational public root certificate or hardware-pinned public key hash used by secure bootloaders and update managers to cryptographically verify the OEM's signature on all incoming software.

assets.name: EVSE Plug & Charge Billing Credentials
assets.description: Provisioning and contract certificates (e.g., ISO 15118) stored within the vehicle's charge controller to negotiate automatic cloud authentication and financial billing at charging stations.

financial.rationale: Credential theft enables widespread automated billing fraud.

assets.name: V2X Cooperative ITS Pseudonym Certificates
assets.description: Short-lived cryptographic certificates used to sign outgoing V2X Cooperative Awareness Messages (CAM), rotating frequently to protect the driver from spatial tracking.

privacy.rationale: Failure of pseudonym rotation allows malicious actors or state entities to track the vehicle's physical movements.

assets.name: Global Positioning & Time Sync Data Flow
assets.description: Live spatial coordinates, time synchronization references, and velocity vectors streaming from satellite receivers to navigation displays and ADAS perception fusion domains.

financial.rationale: Collateral liability and brand damage stemming from mass ADAS misbehavior.

safety.rationale: GNSS spoofing can trick geofenced ADAS features (e.g., highway pilot) into activating in dangerous, unsupported areas.

assets.name: Secure Tunnel & VPN Security Associations
assets.description: Dynamic encryption keys, security parameter indexes (SPI), and network policies maintaining protected telemetry pipelines between the vehicle's edge and OEM backends.

safety.rationale: Compromise of telemetry may delay critical remote safety responses.

assets.name: Firmware Baseline Integrity Manifest / SBOM
assets.description: Cryptographically signed inventory manifests and binary component hashes stored locally to verify the validity of the software stack prior to executing updates.

financial.rationale: Tampering with manifests destroys configuration management, leading to costly fleet-wide audits and compliance failures.

safety.rationale: Could allow a vulnerable component to persist, indirectly increasing safety risks over time.

assets.name: Premium Feature Activation Entitlement Tokens
assets.description: Cryptographic tokens parsed by vehicle gateways or zonal hubs to selectively unlock optional, paid vehicle capabilities (e.g., heated seats, advanced autopilot, increased acceleration).

financial.rationale: Token extraction or bypass leads to massive piracy of post-sale recurring revenue streams.

safety.rationale: Illegally activating unregulated performance features (like acceleration boosts) alters vehicle dynamics.

assets.name: Advanced Diagnostic Authentication Certificates
assets.description: Asymmetric credentials (e.g., ISO 14229-1 AP) used to securely negotiate access rights with authorized dealership and engineering diagnostic tools.

financial.rationale: Loss of these certificates effectively gives threat actors 'god mode' over the entire vehicle fleet.

safety.rationale: Attackers with advanced diagnostic access can actuate physical safety systems remotely.

assets.name: Central High-Performance Compute (HPC) Platform
assets.description: Physical core compute hardware engine housing multi-core asymmetric SoCs, neural processing blocks, and high-speed switching fabrics characteristic of modern Zonal and Consolidated Domain layouts.

safety.rationale: The HPC aggregates multiple safety functions; its failure can cascade across braking, steering, and perception.

assets.name: ADAS / Automated Driving Compute Node
assets.description: High-throughput safety hardware platform hosting perception hardware acceleration matrices, GPUs, and decision-making ASIL-D modules.

safety.rationale: Total loss or compromise of the ADAS hardware instantly disables autonomous driving capabilities and collision avoidance.

assets.name: Cockpit / Infotainment Domain Controller
assets.description: The primary hardware unit driving high-level rich operating systems, passenger displays, connectivity radios, and third-party application spaces.

assets.name: Central Gateway (CGW) Module
assets.description: Physical routing backbone or network processing element orchestrating diagnostics translation and legacy frame conversions across separate structural domain buses.

safety.rationale: A compromised gateway can arbitrarily inject or block safety-critical messages moving between domains.

assets.name: High-Voltage Battery Management System (BMS) Logic
assets.description: Algorithms governing cell balancing, thermal management bounds, and high-voltage contactor isolation for the primary propulsion battery pack.

financial.rationale: Massive warranty costs and reputational destruction due to battery fires.

safety.rationale: Compromise can bypass thermal runaway protections, leading directly to catastrophic battery fires and passenger injury.

assets.name: Powertrain & Chassis Actuation Command Flow
assets.description: High-priority, deterministic network traffic carrying critical steering angle, braking force, and motor torque setpoints to physical control actuators.

safety.rationale: Spoofing or delaying these signals causes unintended acceleration, phantom braking, or loss of steering control.

assets.name: Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) & OBD State Data
assets.description: Data records representing vehicle component health, emissions faults, and active safety system errors, readable via physical tools or remote telemetry.

financial.rationale: Clearing or masking DTCs enables warranty fraud and hides systemic, fleet-wide component failures from the manufacturer.

safety.rationale: Suppressing a critical DTC prevents the driver from servicing a failing safety-critical component before it physically breaks.

assets.name: Zonal Aggregator & Smart I/O Hub Module
assets.description: Physical localized zonal controllers acting as smart concentration nodes, converting local sensor/actuator analog lines directly to Ethernet serialization streams.

safety.rationale: Disabling or hijacking a single zonal hub drops all peripheral traffic for an entire quadrant of the vehicle, potentially blinding ADAS or dropping chassis controls.

assets.name: Real-Time Edge Electronic Control Unit (ECU)
assets.description: Physical dedicated single-function microcontroller handling low-latency loops (characteristic of distributed Classic architectures or physical end-nodes in Zonal layouts).

safety.rationale: Safety severity is highly dependent on whether this edge ECU commands powertrain, chassis, or body elements.

assets.name: Network Time Synchronization Data Flow
assets.description: Precision clock synchronization traffic (gPTP / IEEE 802.1AS) distributed across Ethernet backbones to ensure time-deterministic task scheduling.

financial.rationale: Safety-related crashes due to deterministic network failures lead to massive liability.

safety.rationale: Manipulating or delaying time synchronization messages causes sensor fusion errors and disables time-sensitive ADAS functionality.

assets.name: Intelligent Power Distribution Profile
assets.description: Dynamic configuration matrix and logic parameters handling solid-state eFuse switches over physical zonal power distribution rings.

safety.rationale: Maliciously modifying power configurations allows an attacker to cut supply currents to critical driving systems mid-transit.

assets.name: ECU Calibration Data and Performance Maps
assets.description: Dynamic lookup tables and mechanical alignment boundary constraints flashed during servicing or manufacturing. Manages physical thresholds like engine torque output patterns or camera dead-zone pixels.

safety.rationale: Manipulating calibration maps can trigger uncommanded acceleration or blind safety sensors mid-transit.

assets.name: Secure Debug Challenge-Response Tokens
assets.description: Cryptographic tokens generated on OEM server architectures used by authorized diagnostic tools to temporarily open physical JTAG debug lines or root shells for system remediation.

safety.rationale: Unlocking hardware debug lines enables full platform compromise, allowing an attacker to actuate driving functions.

assets.name: Internal Secure Communication (SecOC / MACsec) Keys
assets.description: Symmetric cryptographic keys (pre-shared or dynamically derived via KEM) used by the vehicle's internal gateways and ECUs to authenticate intra-vehicle CAN traffic (SecOC) or encrypt Ethernet links (MACsec).

safety.rationale: Extraction of these keys completely defeats internal network segmentation, allowing an attacker to seamlessly spoof safety-critical powertrain or chassis messages.

assets.name: System Trusted Certificate Authority (CA) Store
assets.description: The repository of trusted root and intermediate certificates used by the vehicle's operating systems (Linux/QNX/Android) to validate outbound internet connections (TLS) and third-party app signatures.

privacy.rationale: Injecting a rogue CA into this store allows attackers to Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) the vehicle's web traffic, exposing passwords and PII.

assets.name: Powertrain / Vehicle Control Unit (VCU) Logic
assets.description: Core software application binaries managing propulsion energy distribution, torque vectoring, transmission shifting, and motor/engine orchestration.

safety.rationale: Compromise of VCU logic causes uncommanded acceleration, immediate loss of propulsion, or destruction of the drivetrain.

assets.name: Chassis & Dynamic Stability Control Logic
assets.description: Safety-critical application software calculating and commanding anti-lock braking (ABS), electronic stability programs (ESP), and active suspension/steering adjustments.

safety.rationale: Tampering with this software disables braking capabilities or causes erratic, asymmetrical braking forcing a loss of vehicle control.

assets.name: Telematics Communication Routing Daemon
assets.description: The software service running on the TCU or Gateway responsible for managing MQTT/HTTPS connections to the backend and securely parsing/routing authorized cloud commands into the internal vehicle network.

assets.name: CAN / CAN-FD In-Vehicle Network Data Flow
assets.description: Deterministic, frame-based network traffic carrying real-time operational state, diagnostic commands, and control metrics across distributed vehicle domains (Powertrain, Chassis, Body).

safety.rationale: Injecting or starving CAN frames can directly manipulate kinetic actuators or blind the vehicle's internal state monitoring.

assets.name: Automotive Ethernet Backbone Data Flow
assets.description: High-bandwidth, IP-based network traffic forming the backbone of Zonal and HPC architectures, carrying SOA payloads, heavy sensor data (video/LiDAR), and inter-domain communications.

safety.rationale: Flooding the Ethernet backbone prevents ADAS perception data from reaching decision nodes, blinding autonomous systems.

assets.name: Cloud Backend & API Gateway Configuration
assets.description: Infrastructure definitions, API rate limits, load balancer routing rules, and cloud firewall configurations governing the OEM telematics and connected services backend environments.

safety.rationale: Cloud configurations cannot directly bypass physical vehicle safety gateways.

financial.rationale: Misconfiguration allows Denial of Wallet attacks (autoscaling exhaustion) or fleet-wide API scraping.

privacy.rationale: Altering backend routing rules can expose fleet telemetry and user databases to unauthorized endpoints.

assets.name: ADAS Camera, Radar, and LiDAR Sensor Array
assets.description: Physical perception sensors capturing the external vehicle environment to feed machine learning algorithms and active safety functions.

financial.rationale: Hardware recalls or loss of autonomous driving feature subscriptions.

safety.rationale: Compromised or spoofed sensors can cause the vehicle to misinterpret obstacles, leading to collisions.

assets.name: Powertrain & Chassis Electromechanical Actuators
assets.description: Physical edge hardware components (e.g., electronic throttle bodies, brake calipers, steering motors) responsible for actualizing vehicle movement commands.

safety.rationale: Unauthorized manipulation of acceleration, braking, or steering actuators directly results in fatal vehicle behavior.

assets.name: In-Cabin Microphones & Multimedia Capture Devices
assets.description: Physical multimedia peripherals used for voice commands, hands-free calling, and driver monitoring systems.

financial.rationale: Regulatory fines for privacy violations.

privacy.rationale: Eavesdropping on private conversations within the vehicle cabin.
